notes: Applied to a generated ARCHITECTURE_PROPOSAL.md / per-axis artefact. R1-R9 = per-axis variant decision quality; R10-R12 = per-variant design discipline (from real bhph failure modes, see memory feedback_design-research-discipline); R13-R20 = system-completeness layer (from the 12 real scenario-bhph gaps → AWS Well-Architected pillars). R3 is BLOCKING (anti-hallucination). LLM-judge or manual grading; golden bench scenario-bhph is the oracle. Deterministic enforcement of the R13-R20 layer is the COMPLETENESS_COVERAGE audit (FR-12, COMPLETENESS.md ledger) — this rubric judges quality, the audit gates presence.

criteria.name: variant-count
criteria.check: ≥3 variants per axis

criteria.name: concrete-not-vibe
criteria.check: each pro/con is a concrete property (license/benchmark/SLA/cost), not an adjective like 'faster'

criteria.name: anti-hallucination
criteria.check: every technical claim carries [VERIFIED via <source>] or [UNVERIFIED] marker

criteria.name: cost-cited
criteria.check: cost is cited with source OR marked [UNVERIFIED — knowledge cutoff]; never bare confident numbers

criteria.name: honest-recommendation
criteria.check: recommendation states the chosen variant + the ONE driver that broke the tie + 'reconsider if' condition

criteria.name: when-not-to-choose
criteria.check: each variant has an explicit When-NOT-to-choose (KEP Non-Goals pattern)

criteria.name: decision-recorded
criteria.check: chosen decision + rationale recorded in frontmatter/body (пометь выборы и причины)

criteria.name: non-default-variant
criteria.check: ≥1 variant outside the agent's first-instinct popular stack (popular-stack bias mitigation)

criteria.name: progressive-disclosure
criteria.check: new variants surfaced as the user's questions deepen, not all dumped flat (bhph A→F evolution)

criteria.name: failure-modes
criteria.check: each variant enumerates failure modes — crash mid-operation / duplicate side-effect / poison-infinite-retry / race; 'exactly-once delivery' ≠ idempotent side-effect

criteria.name: best-practice-verified
criteria.check: each tech choice verified as vendor-RECOMMENDED [cite doc], not merely 'feasible'; deviations explicitly justified

criteria.name: integration-timing
criteria.check: each external integration checks webhook timeout / required-response-time (respond-immediately if handler slower), sync-vs-async semantics, rate limits, per-resource queue limits

criteria.name: internal-consistency
criteria.check: diagrams/stack-tables/prose do not contradict accepted decisions; a stale artefact after a decision change = fail

criteria.name: flow-completeness
criteria.check: each external integration enumerates inbound handler + outbound call + status/delivery callback + error/timeout path + trigger/scheduler (not just happy inbound)

criteria.name: compliance-privacy
criteria.check: each legal/privacy constraint (opt-out/TCPA, CAN-SPAM, PII at-rest/in-transit) has an explicit enforcement point in the design, not implicit

criteria.name: auth-secrets
criteria.check: each public endpoint declares its authn (webhook signature vs JWT); each secret declares where it is stored and is not exposed (no service-role key in a public function)

criteria.name: observability
criteria.check: where failures / validator-violations surface, the alert channel, and who is paged — not 'monitoring' unbound to a component

criteria.name: data-lifecycle
criteria.check: each growing table/queue/log has a retention or cleanup story (unbounded growth = fail)

criteria.name: cost-quota
criteria.check: per-unit variable cost (LLM/SMS/voice/STT per event) + provider quota/limit checked where a design choice consumes it, flagging when a choice blows budget/quota; evaluate BEFORE locking axes

criteria.name: deploy-ops
criteria.check: local dev (supabase start / functions serve), migration workflow, seed data, staging, CI/CD for functions, backups/PITR are present
